header.no_project: No project yet. Start with a new one.

loading.sub: While you wait, explore the 5-step workflow or tips below.

empty.title: Start a new project
empty.desc:
Just add your meeting notes, and AI picks them up like a senior developer would.
Spec · design · code · review — all 5 steps flow without a break.

team.empty_text: No team projects yet. Create your first team project.

steps.title: 5-Step Shortcuts
steps.desc_plan: Drop in a meeting log and get planning docs automatically
steps.desc_design: Turn needed screens, features and structure into a design
steps.desc_code: Connect AI coding tools + your GitHub repo
steps.desc_lint: Auto-check whether the code matches the design
steps.desc_deliverables: Bundle everything and export at once

strengths.title: You can also do this
strengths.sub: Hidden strengths inside the 5 steps — tap to try them now.
strengths.graph_title: See your design as a graph
strengths.graph_desc: Lay out your system like a map — see what connects to what at a glance.
strengths.lint_desc: AI compares the result against your original plan and flags what's missing.
strengths.obsidian_desc: Export your plan as a linked wiki and keep building it — view it as a graph too.
strengths.rule_desc: Auto-generate rules so AI coding tools build things your way.

sample_confirm.title: Try the sample?
sample_confirm.body: You already have {count} project(s). The sample runs in a separate space called 'Sample Project' — your current work stays safe and untouched.
sample_confirm.note: Creating the sample uses a small amount of AI tokens.

team_dialog.title: New team project
team_dialog.sub: Creating a new project in the {name} team.
team_dialog.note: Once you save a meeting log, it's officially registered as a team project. It stays separate from any personal project with the same name.

onboarding.title: Just upload a meeting log — we handle planning, design, and code for you
onboarding.sub: Nothing complicated to prepare. The 5 steps below flow in order. Take a look around and get started.
onboarding.step1_d: Drop in your meeting as text or audio, as-is.
onboarding.step2_d: We build a planning doc (PRD) capturing the core problems and requirements.
onboarding.step3_d: We draw out which screens, features, and structure you need.
onboarding.step4_d: Get a guide you can drop straight into an AI coding tool.
onboarding.step5_d: Check that the code matches the design, then bundle it into deliverables.

empty_project.title: Pick a project first
empty_project.desc:
From organizing ideas to dev-ready specs and coding rules,
choose a project to work on or create a new one first.
empty_project.hint1: Click the "My Projects" button at the top to pick one quickly
empty_project.hint2: Or type a name in the search box and click "+ New"

hero.tagline:
No coding required. Just jot down what's on your mind, and we'll shape it into a spec AI gets right away.
Hand it to Cursor · Claude as-is, and you get what you wanted — not something off the mark.

compare.sub: Messy thoughts turn into a structured spec & design — the notes on the left become the plan on the right.

raw.line1: want to build an app to track my workout habit
raw.line2: just check off whether I worked out each day
raw.line3: I keep forgetting, so an evening reminder would help
raw.line4: showing my streak would feel rewarding
raw.line5: adding friends to see each other might be motivating
raw.line6: hmm, login… just use Kakao maybe?
raw.line7: would be nice to upload workout photos too
raw.line8: stats too? how many times a week
raw.line9: keep the design clean and simple
raw.line10: that's about all I can think of for now…

item1.title: A spec in minutes
item1.desc: Jot down what's on your mind, and a polished spec lands in your hands in minutes — no agonizing over it.

item2.title: You write zero code
item2.desc: You never write a single line. Harness handles the planning and design; AI coding tools handle the code.

item3.title: Keep the AI you use
item3.desc: Keep using Cursor · Claude · GitHub Copilot just as you do. Nothing new to learn — just hand it over.

item4.title: Start at $0
item4.desc: Start free with no pressure. Upgrade only when you need more — no hidden costs.

flow.sub: From the moment you jot an idea to handing it off to Cursor, it all flows without a snag.
flow.desc_plan: Drop in a stray thought or a meeting note — AI pulls out what matters and tidies it up. Not sure what to write? Start by just talking with the AI.
flow.desc_design: From what you wrote, it auto-draws a 'blueprint' of the screens, features, and data you'll need.
flow.desc_code: Take the plan and design as one bundle to Cursor · Claude, and they build the code you want.
flow.desc_lint: It auto-checks the finished code against your original plan, flagging gaps and fixes.
flow.desc_deliverables: Gather everything your project produced into one handoff-ready ZIP file. You can also export it to a notes app (Obsidian).

faq.headline: Still on the fence?
faq.sub: Answers to the questions we hear most.
faq.q1: I don't know any coding at all — can I still use it?
faq.a1: Yes, that's exactly who we built it for. You don't write code; you jot down what's on your mind, and Harness turns it into a spec AI understands. No jargon needed.
faq.q2: So does it write the code here automatically too?
faq.a2: No — that's where the roles split. Harness handles the planning and design; the actual code is built by AI coding tools like Cursor · Claude. Hand them what Harness organized, and you get what you wanted, not something off the mark.
faq.q3: How long does it take to get a spec?
faq.a3: It depends on how much you write, but usually just a few minutes. A short page of notes gets you an organized spec in 4–6 minutes.
faq.q4: Which AI coding tools can I use it with?
faq.a4: It works great with most tools like Cursor, Claude, and GitHub Copilot. Just copy the plan and design Harness organized as one bundle and hand it over.
faq.q5: Can I use it on a project I've already started?
faq.a5: Yes. Connect your existing GitHub repo and you can jump in right at the code-review step. No need to start over.
faq.q6: Is what I write kept safe?
faq.a6: What you write is used only within your own account, and never for AI training.
faq.q8: Where do I keep working on my exported plan?
faq.a8: The default is a ZIP you hand to your AI coding tool, and you can also export everything as Obsidian notes with wikilinks. Open the graph view in Obsidian to see your plan's structure at a glance and keep growing it.
faq.a7: It's a monthly subscription. Start on the free plan and upgrade when you need more. Your usage so far is always visible at the top of the screen, and on paid plans, even after you hit your limit it switches to Lite mode — a slower but always-available mode — so you never stop.

finale.sub: No coding required. Just write it down, and a spec AI understands is ready in minutes.

pain.sub: If you've felt stuck on your own, it's not a lack of talent — it's a lack of flow.
pain.p1_desc: Your head is full of ideas, but turning them into a spec leaves you unsure where to even start.
pain.p2_desc: Describe it loosely to a developer or AI, and what comes back is nothing like what you wanted.
pain.p3_title: No clear path on your own
pain.p3_desc: Plan, design, code — deciding what to do in what order is hard to figure out alone.

manifesto.body: Most "the AI made something weird" moments are really the result of giving instructions before the plan was sorted out. Harness isn't about piling on more features — it's the one step that turns what's in your head into a structure AI can understand. That one step makes all the difference.

vs.sub: Whether you sort out the plan first or not — that's what decides the outcome.
vs.bad_2: Moving on without knowing what's missing
vs.bad_3: Re-explaining from scratch on every fix
vs.bad_4: Giving up: "AI just doesn't get it"
vs.good_1: Delivered precisely as a structured spec & design
vs.good_2: Screens, features, data — all at a glance
vs.good_3: Auto-checked against the plan, catching gaps
vs.good_4: What you wanted, in one go
